source: Selby et al. 2016; Varfolomeev et al. 2019; Wang et al. 2014; FDA BLA 125554 (nivolumab); FDA BLA 125377 (ipilimumab)
description: Expected findings for immune checkpoint inhibitors. Toxicities ARE the pharmacology: blocking PD-1/CTLA-4 unleashes T-cell activity against self-antigens. ICH S9 HNSTD framework (not traditional NOAEL) is the appropriate regulatory paradigm.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 3 - minimal-mild (grade 1-2) infiltration without tissue destruction is expected pharmacology. Adverse when accompanied by parenchymal degeneration/necrosis or functional organ impairment.
expected_findings.rationale: This IS the intended mechanism of action (T-cell activation). FDA BLA 125554: 'consistent with the pharmacologic activity of the antibody'.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 1 - ALWAYS ADVERSE when present. Myocarditis at any grade represents functional cardiac impairment. 50% mortality rate in affected humans.
expected_findings.rationale: ICI-induced myocarditis in NHPs morphologically recapitulates the human disease. Most clinically significant ICI toxicity.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 2, or any grade with clinical signs (diarrhea, bloody stool) or crypt destruction
expected_findings.rationale: CTLA-4 is critical for gut immune tolerance. CTLA-4 knockout mice develop fatal enterocolitis.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 2, or any grade with endocrine function changes (T4 decrease, TSH increase) or glandular destruction
expected_findings.rationale: Thyroid is the most commonly affected endocrine organ in ICI therapy.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 2, or any grade with ALT/AST >3x baseline or hepatocellular necrosis
expected_findings.rationale: Immune-mediated hepatitis reflects loss of hepatic immune tolerance.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 3, or any grade with epidermal necrosis, ulceration, or severe pruritus with self-mutilation
expected_findings.rationale: Skin irAEs are the most common but typically least severe checkpoint inhibitor toxicity.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 2, or any grade with BUN/creatinine elevation or tubular degeneration
expected_findings.rationale: T-cell-mediated tubulointerstitial nephritis.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 2, or any grade causing functional impairment
expected_findings.rationale: Part of the multi-organ irAE pattern. Salivary glands are common autoimmune targets (Sjogren's syndrome).

expected_findings.description: Splenic follicular hypertrophy / marginal zone expansion
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 3, or any grade with massive splenomegaly or hypersplenism
expected_findings.rationale: Splenic immune activation is a direct pharmacological consequence of checkpoint blockade.

expected_findings.description: Lymph node changes (germinal center depletion or hyperplasia)
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Grade 3, or any grade with massive lymphadenopathy or lymphoproliferative disorder
expected_findings.rationale: Lymph node changes reflect altered T-cell trafficking, not pathology per se.

expected_findings.description: Increased CD4+/CD8+ activated T cells in blood
expected_findings.typical_magnitude: 1.2-2.0x baseline total lymphocytes; CD8+ effector memory 2-5x
expected_findings.rationale: This IS the intended pharmacodynamic effect. Non-adverse as isolated finding.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >2x baseline or accompanied by autoantibody-mediated pathology
expected_findings.rationale: Enhanced T-helper activity provides increased B-cell help, driving polyclonal immunoglobulin production.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: <50% baseline or accompanied by clinical edema/effusions
expected_findings.rationale: Negative acute-phase protein response during immune activation. May also reflect GI protein loss from colitis.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: ALWAYS ADVERSE when confirmed elevated above assay reference range. Sentinel biomarker for ICI myocarditis.
expected_findings.rationale: Any troponin elevation indicates cardiomyocyte damage.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >3x baseline with concurrent troponin elevation
expected_findings.rationale: Co-elevation of troponin-I and NT-pro-BNP is the diagnostic biomarker pattern for ICI myocarditis.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >5x baseline or accompanied by band forms (left shift)
expected_findings.rationale: Neutrophilia is a secondary response to tissue inflammation, not a direct pharmacological effect.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Extreme eosinophilia or accompanied by eosinophilic tissue infiltration
expected_findings.rationale: Eosinophilia reflects Th2 component of dysregulated immune response.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: <50% baseline with concurrent TSH elevation
expected_findings.rationale: Functional hypothyroidism from immune-mediated thyroid destruction.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >3x baseline with concurrent T4 decrease
expected_findings.rationale: Compensatory pituitary feedback response to decreased circulating thyroid hormones.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >3x baseline or accompanied by hepatocellular necrosis
expected_findings.rationale: Unlike oligonucleotide ALT elevation, ICI ALT reflects direct immune-mediated hepatocyte destruction.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >3x baseline
expected_findings.rationale: Parallels ALT pattern in immune-mediated hepatitis.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >1.5x baseline with concurrent renal histopathology or creatinine elevation
expected_findings.rationale: Reduced glomerular filtration from immune-mediated renal inflammation.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >1.5x baseline with concurrent renal histopathology
expected_findings.rationale: Reduced GFR from immune-mediated tubulointerstitial inflammation.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: >50% increase or accompanied by hypersplenism
expected_findings.rationale: Enhanced lymphocyte activation and proliferation from checkpoint blockade.
